ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”

translated: “O Lord, I am your servant, I am weak, and I have no strength, but I will praise your name, and I will tell of your greatness.”

Dina n-ni chɛ ka m baŋ ni a niŋ n dan’ maa nirilim maŋli.”
translated: He said to them, “I have a dream, and it is this: I will go and bring my brother’s son, and he will be king in my place.” And he said, “Later, when I am gone, you will choose a leader from among yourselves, and he will be king.” This is what he said before he died, and his name was Yisahaku. And so they chose a leader from among themselves, and they made him king.

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Di saha ka Reechɛl daa yɛli Yaakubu, “N dab’ bipuɣiŋga Biliha m-bɔŋɔ; zaŋm’ o dɔni ka o dɔɣi bihi ti ma, ka n zaŋ ba ʒili n naba zuɣu.”

translated: The person who does not know God, he is like a lost sheep, and he will be punished by God. He will be destroyed with his wealth and children.

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Nuhu zuliya m-bɔŋɔ: Nuhu daa nyɛla wuntizɔra ŋun jɛmdi Naawuni, ka ka galimi o ʒiɛman’ ni.
translated: Nuhu was a righteous man: Nuhu was a faithful servant of God, and he warned his people.

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.

translated: A long time ago, there was a man who had a very beautiful wife. He loved her very much, and she was very kind to him. One day, the man went to Lebanon to trade, and he left his wife at home. While he was away, a wicked man came and seduced his wife.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: When he arrived there, he said, "The Lord is my shepherd!" Then he built an altar there and called it Mahanaim.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ka binyɛra din bindi tiŋgban’ ni ka mali biɛlim mini tihi din wala mali kpila balibu kam zaa shiri bili. Ka Naawuni daa nya li ka di viɛla.
translated: When the world was created, God put humans and animals in it. God then said that humans should take care of the world.

translated: The one who is truly wise is the one who knows how to share what they have with others, and the one who knows how to help those in need, even if it means sacrificing their own comfort.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ka Naawuni daa nam mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri balibu kam zaa, ka nam niɣi balibu kam zaa, ka nam binnɛma bɛn gbamdi tiŋa balibu kam zaa. Ka Naawuni daa nya ba ka bɛ viɛla.
translated: When God created the world, He created the sun, the moon, and the stars. He also created the earth and everything on it. And God saw that it was good.

ref: Lamech and Adah had two sons, Jabal and Jubal. Their son Jabal was the first to live in tents and raise sheep and goats. Jubal was the first to play harps and flutes.

text: Ka Yawɛ ŋun nyɛ Naawuni ŋɔ daa yɛli, “Nyama, pumpɔŋɔ ninsal’ bemi kaman ti ni be shɛm, ka mi zaɣ’ suŋ ni zaɣ’ biɛɣu, ka di pa shɛli ka o ti pɔhigi nyɛvili tia la walli di m-be hal ni saha din ka bahigu.”
translated: When God created man, He said, “Look, I have given you all the plants and trees bearing fruit, and all the grains, and I have given you animals to eat, and everything that moves on the earth, and all the fish in the sea. They are all yours to use as food.”

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Ka Naawuni daa lan yɛli, “Binnɛma balibu kam, bɛnya tiŋgbani zuɣu; niɣi mini binnɛma bɛn gbamdi tiŋa ni mɔɣu ni biŋkɔbiri balibu kam.” Ka di shiri niŋ lala.
translated: When the Creator finished speaking, He said, “Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear.” And it was so.
